ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yesu gi dii loo gikolii dɔ mɔ‑rɛ mɔ-akasɛbo mɔ, ne mɛ kyon.
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, “I am going away, but I will come back.”

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
text: Yesu gi tɔgɛ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Gɛnɔɔbono fɛ laa nyɛ Wurubuaarɛ gɛwuragyi dɔ akyenabi mɔ i dɛ fɛɛ gikpalɛ gibono nan baa da faa. Awuratɔ gbaaꞌ ako a ŋara ndɔɔ nko dɔ, ne ɔnyen ɔko gi kpaa beendi amɔ. I tansi wɔra mɔ ɔkon gikyɔ, ne ɔ lɔrɔ amɔ ŋara, ne ɔ kpaa fɛ ilaa kamaasɛ ibono ɔ bo mɔ, ne o kii kpaa sɔɔ gɛsinkpan gɛbono dɔ awuratɔ mɔ a bo mɔ.”
translated: Jesus told them this parable: “No one tears a piece from a new garment and sews it on an old garment; otherwise the new will be torn, and the piece from the new will not match the old. And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the new wine will burst the skins and will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed. But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.”

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Imɔso sa fo-asogya gɛnɔ yɛɛ mɛꞌ kpaa dii mɔ-gɛkyan mɔ dandan kaaborɛ nkɛ nsa mɔ giꞌ lɔɔ gyanꞌ. Iŋ gyɛ gɛnen mɔ, mɔ-akasɛbo mɔ mɛ laa naa kpaa yuuri mɔ-gibuni mɔ de mɛꞌ kyu naa mɛ kya kɛɛ abon yɛɛ Wurubuaarɛ gi kyingi mɔ lii ibuni dɔ. Gɛnen gɛmara-gɛmara abon abono a laa wɔra nyɛnyɛn don abono mɔmɔ-ɔbelɛnsɛ mɔ gbaa-gbaa tɛ de ansi ne ɔ kɛɛ mɔ.”
translated: One day a certain man went to the bush to set a trap and he caught a very large antelope. When he saw it, the hunter was very happy and he carried the animal home to show his children so that they might know that God had blessed them with meat. All the people of the village came together and they celebrated greatly because of that single animal.

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Yesu gi lɛɛ gɛnɔ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Gimaadena mɔ iŋ gyɛ nyamesɛ kamaasɛ ne nan taalɛ kyena, gɛnen mɔ, abono Wurubuaarɛ gi kyu sa mɔmɔ mɔ wolɛ.
translated: Jesus looked at him and said, “You are Simon, son of John; you will be called Cephas (which means Peter).

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Sakpii tɛ kyaabɔɔ mɔ. Imɔso sakpii mɔ dɔ ako mɛ tɔgɛ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Fo-nyi de fo-tedɛana mɛ yelɛ nŋmaŋman mɛ kya laarɛ mɛꞌ wu fo.”
translated: The child went to the farm. When the child got there he met the spider who said to him, “Your mother sent me to tell you that she wants you to come home.”

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Fɛꞌ nu yɛɛ Wurubuaarɛ laa lɛɛ ansi taa fɛye-ɔsowolɛ yɛgɛ sa fɛye.
translated: The man who was Wurubuaarɛ went to the farm to look for his hoe.

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
text: ne ɔbono ɔ kya laarɛ ɔꞌ wɔra fɛye-wura mɔ, i kaaborɛ ɔꞌ wɔra mɔ-nyoro fɛɛ fɛye-gɛbide.
translated: When a person sees that he has become an old man, he remembers that he was once a young boy.

ref: Jesus knew what they were up to, and he said, “Why are you trying to test me? Show me a coin!”
text: Yesu gi dɛsɛ yɛɛ mɛ kerɛ ansi daa ne mɛ kya taasɛ mɔ. Imɔso ɔ taasɛ mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Menɛ so ne fɛ kya soo me idoo gɛnen? Ɔko ɔꞌ lɛɛ aterenbi gibi ba de nꞌ kerɛ.”
translated: Jesus told a parable saying, “A certain man planted a vineyard and rented it to some farmers. At harvest time he sent a servant to them to collect some of the fruit of the vineyard.”

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: Asafo mɔ mɛ nu Wura Hɛrodɛ ilaa tɔgɛsɛ mɔ, ne mɛ kyon. Mɛ kya kpe mɔ, mɛ kii wu gigyɛkpebi gibono mɛ wolaa wu owi ɔbaaliiten so mɔ gi gyangbara gi sa ɔmɔ. Aberɛ abono gi fo nfono gɛbii mɔ gɛ bo mɔ, gi yelɛ.
translated: When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he inquired of them where the Christ was to be born. So they said to him.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: Yesu gi kii terɛ sakpii ɔbono ɔ bo nfono mɔ ba mɔ asɛ ne ɔ tɔgɛ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛye pɛwu fɛꞌ yela giso de fɛꞌ nu gɛsɛ yɛɛ
translated: Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you; as the Father has sent me, so I also send you.”

ref: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
text: Ɔkyobo ɔbono, mɔ, ɔ baa duu ifɛ mɔ ne n gyɛ gɛnen Ɔbonsan, ilaa nyɛnyɛn pɛwu gɛmu mɔ. Awayu mɔ gigbaa owi mɔ ne n gyɛ owi ɔbono gɛsinkpan so gɛkyena gɛ laa yela ɔkara mɔ. Adega abono mɛ laa gbaa awayu mɔ ne n gyɛ Wurubuaarɛ dɔ isɔɔ.
translated: The old man, when he arrived at that place, was not alone, Obonsan, he was carrying a heavy load. The child who was following him was not his own child, but rather a child he had taken from someone else. The old woman also took the child and gave him to God.

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu gi nu ɔkalan ɔbono mɛ baa tɔgɛ Ɔbelɛnsɛ Gyarasɛ mɔ, o kisee tɔgɛ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Gɛŋ sa foꞌ selɛ gifuu. Fo berɛ, kpɛ sɔɔ me gyi daa.”
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd following him, he said to Philip, “Where shall we buy bread for these people to eat?”

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Yesu mɔ‑rɛ mɔmɔ mɛ naa kpaa loo Kapɛɛniyon. Mɛ bo nno mɔ, mɔmɔ-gɛkɛ kyoolasɛ gɛ fo. Asa mɛ kpaa gyan mɔmɔ-gikyangbon dɔ, ne Yesu gi nyiile mɔmɔ Wurubuaarɛ ilaa nno.
translated: Jesus saw two brothers, Simon called Peter and his brother Andrew. They were casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen. At once they left their nets and followed Jesus.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: Ndɔɔ mɔ ne n gyɛ gɛsinkpan. Awayu mɔ ne n gyɛ asa abono mɛ kya nyɛ ɔkyenaten Wurubuaarɛ gɛwuragyi dɔ mɔ, ne ifɛ mɔ, mɔ, ne n gyɛ asa abono mɛ bo Ɔbonsan, nyamesɛ nyɛnyɛn mɔ gikpen dɔ mɔ.
translated: This world is not a place to settle. The journey of life is like a boat crossing to the other side of the river to reach Wurubuaarɛ’s dwelling place, and its beauty is like a boat heading to the Creator, where all living things will finally arrive.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Nfono mɔ dɔɔbo mɔ adega mɔ mɛ baa taasɛ mɔ yɛɛ, ‘Ɔbelɛnsɛ, iŋ gyɛ awayu ne fo duu fo-ndɔɔ mɔ dɔ? Fonɛ dɔ ne ifɛ idɛ ɔnan i lii?’
translated: The owner of the yam farm went and stood before him and said, ‘Ɔbelɛnsɛ, is it true that you have taken your own daughter to the farm? Is that what people say you have done?’

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Ne Yesu gi kyɛ asa alɔbi kpɛi-kpɛi gikyɔ ne ɔ kosorɛ ilaa nyɛnyɛn oduduu kpɛi-kpɛi lii asa so kee gikyɔ. Ilaa nyɛnyɛn oduduu mɔ i nyi Yesu, imɔso Yesu mɛŋ sa imɔ ɔkpa de iꞌ nyɛ tɔngɛ. Yesu Gi Tɔgɛ Wurubuaarɛ Ɔkalan Konkonsɛ Mɔ Galeli
translated: Jesus went up a high mountain to pray by himself, and his appearance was completely changed on that mountain. His appearance became dazzling, and Jesus was talking with Moses and Elijah. Jesus is the Son of God Almighty from Galilee.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: ne Yesu gi yii mɔ. O yii mɔ mɔ, ɔkyagyii mɔ gi taa mɔ yɛgɛ, ne ɔ taalɛ kii koso keda mɔ-gisafo.
translated: Jesus said this. He said that if a person wants to follow him, that person must first deny himself.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Yesu gi lɛɛ gɛnɔ sa mɔmɔ yɛɛ, “N kya tɔgɛ fɛye gɛsintin, nengyene fɛ sɔɔ Wurubuaarɛ gyi ne fɛŋ sɔɔ fɛye-nyoro akyɔɔlɛ fɛye-asen dɔ mɔ, fɛ laa wɔra ilaa ibono nɛ kyu wɔra oyii baarɛ faa mɔ ɔnan. Ne i mɛŋ gyɛ imɔ ibono wolɛ gbaa. Nkana fɛ laa taalɛ tɔgɛ gibii gidɛ yɛɛ, ‘Koso gɛrɛ kpaa tɔrɔ apoo dɔ,’ gɛsintin mɔ, i laa wɔra gɛnen.
translated: Jesus told them this parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field, but while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and went away. When the wheat sprouted and formed heads, then the weeds also appeared. The owner’s servants came to him and said, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?’”

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Ne Yesu gi diirɛ mɔ-ansi kerɛ soso ne ɔ muusi. Ɔ kisee kerɛ ɔnyen mɔ ne ɔ tɔgɛ de mɔ-gɛdɛ dɔ yɛɛ “Ɛfata”.
translated: Jesus took the deaf man aside, away from the crowd, and touched his ears and tongue and said, “Ephphatha.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Yesu gi lɛɛ gɛnɔ sa ɔmɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛye-aso dɔ i kɔrɔ ifuu so daa ne Mosisi gi sa fɛye ɔkpa de fɛꞌ kine fɛye-kaana gɛnen. Nkana lii gɛsɛ de gɛsɛ mɔ, i mɛŋ dɛ gɛnen.
translated: Jesus said to them again, “I am the gate; whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out, and find pasture.

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Nfono mɔ ɔnyen mɔ aso mɔ a tigi, ne ɔ san ɔ kya nu ilaa, ne mɔ-ɔnandii kee gi sangɛ ɔ san ɔ kya tɔngɛ dandan.
translated: The child who is disobedient, when he grows up, he will not listen to advice, and his children too will not listen to instruction at all.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Imɔso fɛꞌ kpaa yɛgɛ gɛsinkpan so asa kpɛi-kpɛi pɛwu mɛꞌ bingiri me-akasɛbo de fɛꞌ kyu aye-sɛ Wurubuaarɛ ginyen, de me, mɔ-bi mɔ ginyen, de mɔ-Oduduu mɔ ginyen kyu gyere gɛnen asa abono Wurubuaarɛ sagyere,
translated: The reason the people of the earth are in great suffering today is because they have forsaken the word of Wurubuaarɛ, and I, my people, and the people of Oduduu have also turned away from following the word of Wurubuaarɛ.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Fɛ kya laarɛ asa abono mɛ kya laarɛ fɛye-ilaa wolɛ mɔ, nnɛ so ne Wurubuaarɛ laa tɛɛla fɛye? Lɛnpoo asɔɔbo abono mɛ kya kudi asa gyi mɔ gbaa kee mɛ kya laarɛ daa abono mɛ kya laarɛ mɔmɔ-ilaa mɔ.
translated: If it is good for a person to do good to others, is it not better to do it for God? A poor person who helps others is better than a rich person who does not help anyone.

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Nꞌ tɔgɛ fɛye yɛɛ Ilaagya gi ti wolaa kii ba, ne ibono Gyuda awura mɛ kya laarɛ mɔ mɛ ti kyu wɔra mɔ fɛɛ gɛnɔɔbono mɛ wolaa ŋmarasɛ wɔra Wurubuaarɛ agyɛbi mɔ dɔ mɔ.” Yesu Gi Kosorɛ Ilaa Nyɛnyɛn Oduduu Gɛbii Gɛko So
translated: And he said to them, “Thus it is written, that the Christ should suffer and on the third day rise from the dead, and that repentance for the forgiveness of sins should be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.” Jesus the Resurrection and the Life

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Ne ɔŋ dɛ gɛnen mɔ, nnɛ ne ɔ dɛ? Fɛ wu o bun gikuru dɛnsɛ? Kuaa, nkana akuru dɛnsɛ abunbo ne fɛ kya laarɛ de fɛꞌ kpaa kerɛ berɛ mɔ, ɔfaanan fɛ kpe daa awura-awura nwi.
translated: If you do not know that person, do you know him? Have you not heard the old story? Well, the elders say that if you want to find that path, you must follow the footprints left in the sand.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.

translated: The horse owner and the Jew owner had a dispute about a path that the horse owner used to let his horse go to drink at the river, and so he refused to let the Jew's donkey use the same path.

text: Ne fɛŋ ti kalɛ Wurubuaarɛ agyɛbi dɔ nfono mɛ ŋmarasɛ Wurubuaarɛ asunbi alɛɛbo gɛsun wɔrasɛ ilaa mɔ? Nno i kya nyiile yɛɛ Wurubuaarɛ asunbi alɛɛbo mɔ mɛ bo mɔmɔ-gɛsun wɔrasɛ mɛ kya wɔra Wurubuaarɛ ɔson obu mɔ dɔ gɛkɛ kyoolasɛ, ne Wurubuaarɛ mɛŋ kya kyu imɔ yɛɛ mɛ tɔrɔ gɛkɛ kyoolasɛ nbara.
translated: Why did Wurubuaarɛ allow the bad person to kill his child, instead of letting the child live so that Wurubuaarɛ himself could punish the evil one, since Wurubuaarɛ does not want anyone to take vengeance on his behalf?

text: Yesu gi wu gɛnen mɔ, i loo mɔ. Ne ɔ tɔgɛ sa mɔ-akasɛbo mɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛꞌ yɛgɛ nbii mɔ giꞌ ba me asɛ; fɛŋꞌ baa tii mɔmɔ-ɔkpa. Abono mɛ dɛ fɛɛ gɛnen nbii ndɛana ɔnan mɔ ne n kya nyɛ Wurubuaarɛ gɛwuragyi dɔ akyenabi mɔ.
translated: Jesus said to them, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me. If you had known me, you would have known my Father also; from now on you know him and have seen him.”

text: Yesu mɔ‑rɛ mɔ-akasɛbo mɔ mɛ kpe Kapɛɛniyon mɔ, Wurubuaarɛ ɔson obu mɔ too-too asɔɔbo ako mɛ ba Piita asɛ, ne mɛ taasɛ mɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛye-ɔbelɛnsɛ kya too Wurubuaarɛ ɔson obu mɔ too-too ne?”

translated: Jesus said to them, “I am the light of the world. The Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to arrest him, but he escaped from their hands.”

text: Yesu san ɔ kya nyiile ɔmɔ ilaa mɔ, ɔ tɔgɛ yɛɛ, “Ɔko mɛŋ bo no ɔ laa nyɔga fatela kyu loo obu dɔ de oꞌ kyu fɛɛ gɛlɔbi baala bun mɔ so, abɛɛ oꞌ kyu wɔra npa gɛsɛ. Ɔ laa kyu mɔ gyanꞌ daa nfono i laa wu asa mɔ.

translated: Jesus was praying in a certain place, and when he finished, one of his disciples said to him, “Lord, teach us to pray, as John taught his disciples.”

translated: When they had come, they said, “We have seen his star in the east and have come to worship the king of the Jews. We have seen his star rise and have come to bring him gifts. So now we want to see him and give him these gifts, for we know that he is the one born to be king.”

text: Mɔ-ilaa tɔgɛsɛ mɔ dɔ mɔ, ɔ kya tɔgɛ yɛɛ, “Nkɛ nbono Wurubuaarɛ gi yela mɔ gi fo. Idɛ kon ne Wurubuaarɛ gɛwuragyi dɔ akyenabi a laa ba asa dɔ ne. Imɔso fɛꞌ nu fɛye-nyoro gɛsɛ de fɛꞌ kyɛɛgɛ de fɛꞌ sɔɔ Wurubuaarɛ ɔkalan konkonsɛ mɔ gyi.”

ref: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
text: Yesu gi koso lii nno, ne o kpe Gyudiya gɛsinkpan so de Gyɔɔdan ɔbon mɔ gibenbɛ giko so. Sakpii gi ti kpaa kyaabɔɔ mɔ, ne o nyiile mɔmɔ ilaa fɛɛ gɛnɔɔbono ɔ wolaa ɔ kya wɔra mɔ.
translated: Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, and they laid him in a manger because there was no room for them in the inn. The shepherds were in the fields nearby, keeping watch over their flocks at night.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
text: Ne Yesu gi lɛɛ gɛnɔ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Fɛ san wu gɛnen mɔ, fɛŋꞌ baa gya mɔ. Ɔbono ɔ dɛ me-ginyen ɔ kya wɔra amunale ilaa mɔ, gɛnen kaasɛ ɔbono maŋ baa kii tɔgɛ ilaa nyɛnyɛn iko kyu lii me so ayaa abono so.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “If anyone wants to follow me, let him deny himself. Whoever loves his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Fɛ laa nu ikɔ so titiritii de koo-koo. Fɛ kya nu gɛnen mɔ, fɛ mɛŋ sa fɛꞌ yɛgɛ fɛye-gisen giꞌ ŋmaa fɛye. Imɔ idɛ i gyɛ ilaa ibono i laa ba daa. Ne i mɛŋ gyɛ yɛɛ ilaa kamaasɛ i kya yela ɔkara ayaa abono so ne.
translated: They went to the farm very early in the morning. When they reached the farm, they did not find their yam vines on the mounds. What had happened was that goats had already gone there. So they realized that goats had eaten their yam vines.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Ndɛ nkɛ nsa ne asa gikpen belɛ gidɛ mɛ buu me faa, mɛ mɛŋ baa mɛ bo agyudɔ de mɛꞌ gyi. Imɔso mɔmɔ-ilaa i bo me ayen.
translated: When the sun rises and the cock crows, I wake up and go to the farm, I don’t go anywhere else. I only go there every day.

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: O yii gɛgbɛ mɔ gɛnen mɔ, Yesu gi kisee kerɛ. O wu mɔ mɔ, ɔ tɔgɛ sa mɔ yɛɛ, “Me-bi, yɛgɛ fo-gisen gi tɔrɔ fo! Gisɔɔgyi gibono fo bo sa me mɔ ne nan kyɛ fo.” Ayaa abono so mɔ, ɔkyii mɔ gilɔ mɔ gi ta.
translated: When he saw the crowd, Jesus felt sorry for them. He said to his disciples, “My heart is moved with pity for these people; they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away hungry, lest they faint on the way.”

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Ne Yesu gi kyɛ asa alɔbi kpɛi-kpɛi gikyɔ ne ɔ kosorɛ ilaa nyɛnyɛn oduduu kpɛi-kpɛi lii asa so kee gikyɔ. Ilaa nyɛnyɛn oduduu mɔ i nyi Yesu, imɔso Yesu mɛŋ sa imɔ ɔkpa de iꞌ nyɛ tɔngɛ. Yesu Gi Tɔgɛ Wurubuaarɛ Ɔkalan Konkonsɛ Mɔ Galeli
translated: Jesus went up on a high mountain by himself to pray. As he was praying, his appearance changed and his clothes became dazzling white. Jesus the Transfiguration of the Lord on Mount Tabor

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Sakpii belɛ gi buu mɔ. Mɛ loo nno mɔ, ɔ kyɛ kyɛ abono mɛ kya lɔ mɔ.
translated: The child is crying because of hunger. The mother is not there, he is just crying and crying.
